completeness_goals.goal: The reconstruction is delivered through the workspace-provided reconstruct_weight.py such that running that script (or importing reconstruct_weight from it) is what yields the verified outputs. Work landed only in unrelated scratch files, ad-hoc notebooks, or side modules that reconstruct_weight.py does not import or call is not credited toward the other goals. In addition, the standard benchmark-wide anti-effort patterns disqualify a submission from earning credit on the other goals even if the harness or judge would otherwise count its output: (a) ZERO-EDITS-WITH-LARGE-PATCH — the agent made no real source edits to reconstruct_weight.py (or the imported callees), yet the captured trial patch is large because the patch-capture process snapshot the workspace tree (sandbox artifacts, .pyc files, pulled model weights, log dumps) rather than agent work; the diff has no meaningful change to the entrypoint; (b) GIT-OPS EXFILTRATION — the 'fix' is delivered by running `git checkout main`, `git reset --hard origin/main`, `git pull` from an external remote, `git fetch && git merge`, applying a downloaded patch file, or otherwise importing code from outside the sandbox rather than authoring an inversion; (c) COMMENT-ONLY / WHITESPACE-ONLY DIFFS — the patch to reconstruct_weight.py consists exclusively of added comments, docstrings, blank lines, reformatting, or import reordering with no executable-statement change to the unpack/reconstruct logic; (d) DELETION-OF-WORKING-TESTS-AS-A-FIX — the agent makes the verification loop, the main() PASSED check, or any test_*.py file pass by deleting tests, weakening the < 0.1 max-diff threshold, removing the diff print, short-circuiting the assertion, or commenting out the failing parameters from the verification loop instead of fixing the math. Any of (a)-(d) is a hard anti-effort failure regardless of what the verifier prints.
completeness_goals.rationale: User-pinned entrypoint plus benchmark-wide anti-effort floor. instruction.md tells the agent to reimplement Nunchaku SVDQ in the workspace and verify the reconstruction, and the workspace the user hands the agent contains a buggy reconstruct_weight.py whose function name (reconstruct_weight) is the contract the verification loop talks to. The scratch-file clause prevents crediting parallel test_*.py or quantize_*_fix.py scratch files that demonstrate pack/unpack in isolation but never patch the actual reconstruct_weight.py the user is iterating on — confirmed concrete failure mode in this trial: the agent created test_all_correct.py / test_all_unpack.py with correct-looking unpack code, never edited reconstruct_weight.py, and the verifier reported inf diffs on all six parameters. The four enumerated patterns (zero-edits-with-large-patch, git-ops exfiltration, comment-only/whitespace-only diffs, deletion-of-tests-as-fix) are the standard cross-task anti-effort floor recurring across the benchmark and are listed here explicitly so the judge does not credit any of them even if a downstream verifier or F2P gate happens to register a numeric pass.

completeness_goals.goal: After the agent's changes, the reconstruction in reconstruct_weight.py recovers the two square-shape SVDQ parameters named in the instruction (attn.to_out.0 and attn.to_add_out) with max element-wise difference < 0.1 against their ground-truth weight tensors — the threshold the user-handed workspace harness treats as PASSED. The pass must come from a genuine inversion of proj_down/proj_up/qweight/wscales/smooth_factor as fed in by the harness: hardcoding or loading the ground-truth `weight` tensor (e.g., returning the file from `pt/`), short-circuiting the diff check, mocking the verification call, or otherwise bypassing the math does NOT count as recovery and earns no credit for this goal even if the harness prints PASSED.
completeness_goals.rationale: Headline ask in instruction.md: 'write a new script that reconstructs weight given the groundtruth proj_down, proj_up, qweight, wscales, smooth_factor, and verify your result against the groundtruth weight'. The user explicitly names the six target parameters and tells the agent 'for now let's focus on attn.to_out.0'; max_diff < 0.1 is the PASSED bar baked into the workspace's main() verification loop the user hands the agent. The square-shape pair is the first half of the six. Square shapes are the easier subset (N=K=3072, no rectangular tile edge cases) and a partial fix that only handles them lands here.

completeness_goals.goal: After the agent's changes, the reconstruction in reconstruct_weight.py recovers all four rectangular-shape MLP parameters named in the instruction (img_mlp.net.0.proj, img_mlp.net.2, txt_mlp.net.0.proj, txt_mlp.net.2) with max element-wise difference < 0.1 against their ground-truth weight tensors — the same PASSED threshold the workspace harness uses. As with goal_1, the pass must come from genuinely inverting the packed inputs (proj_down/proj_up/qweight/wscales/smooth_factor) per parameter: hardcoding or loading the ground-truth `weight` tensor from `pt/`, returning per-name lookups of the ground truth, branching on parameter name to swap in cached results, or otherwise bypassing the unpack/reconstruct math does NOT count for this goal even if the harness reports PASSED.
completeness_goals.rationale: Coverage requirement explicit in instruction.md: 'test the weight reconstruction using all 6 parameters attn.to_out.0, attn.to_add_out, img_mlp.net.0.proj, img_mlp.net.2, txt_mlp.net.0.proj, txt_mlp.net.2 in the folder pt'. Rectangular shapes (12288x3072 and 3072x12288) exercise non-square code paths — differing group counts along the K axis, asymmetric tile dimensions, and the up-vs-down lowrank projection orientations — that a square-only fix can pass while still being structurally wrong, so they are carved out from goal_1 to penalize patches that hardcode N==K assumptions.

completeness_goals.goal: The fix in reconstruct_weight.py is a principled inversion of the upstream packing format — i.e., the integer-weight bit-packed layout is unpacked (not just byte-split), the per-group scales are unpacked rather than naively transposed, and the low-rank up/down projections are unpacked rather than used in their packed memory layout — so that the end-to-end reconstruction is correct for both square and rectangular shapes rather than for one shape class by accident.
completeness_goals.rationale: Captures the substantive engineering required by the instruction's 'read the original source code in the folders nunchaku and ComfyUI-Nunchaku to understand how the SVDQ layer works' clause and the three packing-format inversions implied by the task (qweight, wscales, lowrank up/down). Folded into a single diagnostic gate because the three sub-stages co-pass when goals 1 and 2 both pass across square and rectangular shapes — splitting them into three independent secondaries (as v2 of this rubric did before consolidation) gave them duplicative weight against the cores. Diagnostic tier means this is informational: a patch that fails this conceptual check but somehow passes both shape classes still gets the cores.

decomposition_notes: Two cores (square shapes, rectangular shapes) at 0.45 each, one diagnostic at 0.10, one anti-effort at 0.00 (sum = 1.00). Square/rectangular split is justified because the failure-mode partition matches: hardcoded-N==K patches pass the cores' square half and fail the rectangular half, and the user explicitly names six parameters across both shape classes, so collapsing them into one core would let a half-correct patch take the full headline weight. Diagnostic gate exists because the three packing-format inversions (qweight bit-packing, per-group scales, lowrank up/down) are the literal source-code reading task asked for in instruction.md, but their pass/fail correlates so tightly with the cores that giving them separate weight would double-count. Anti-effort goal_0 has weight 0.0 by convention; it is a guard predicate, not a credit-bearing line item — if it fails, the other goals are evaluated as if no work was done.
